race.name: GFNY República Dominicana
race.tagline: Be a Pro For A Day in Paradise

climate.description: Warm weather racing in March with tropical conditions. Punta Cana features consistent warm temperatures and Caribbean trade winds that significantly impact the course.

terrain.surface: Rolling hills with distinct character changes. First 47km relatively flat with rolling hills and favorable wind. Middle section features rural hinterland with rolling hills (4-6 degree grades). Final sections return to main roads with headwind challenges.
features: Rolling hills with distinct character changes
First 47km relatively flat with rolling hills and favorable wind
Middle section features rural hinterland with rolling hills (4-6 degree grades)
Final sections return to main roads with headwind challenges

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64.

course_description.character: A wind-dependent Caribbean cycling challenge featuring a deceptively fast outbound leg with favorable tailwind through rural tropical terrain, followed by a punishing headwind return that tests mental toughness and pacing discipline. The course transitions from coastal roads to lush hinterland and back, with the Caribbean Sea and impressive hill backdrops providing scenic contrast.
course_description.signature_challenge: The wind reversal from outbound to return leg. Riders must exercise extreme restraint during the fast, favorable outbound section (km 0-67) despite the pace feeling easy and the field moving quickly. The return journey's headwind separates those who paced conservatively from those who burned matches early.
suffering_zones: Headwind section returning to Punta Cana. All favorable wind from outbound leg becomes direct opposition. Riders face 20km of headwind before wind shifts to left side.
Last 10km approaching finish. Wind from left side with opportunity to attack, but fatigue and wind resistance create final selection point.
For medium course riders, the 47km turnaround means immediate headwind for final 47km to finish. Wind challenge is proportionally harder for shorter distance.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Extensive all-inclusive resort options in Bavaro and Punta Cana area (La Costa del Coco). Wide range of luxury accommodations available.
logistics.transport: Punta Cana is the second most popular tourist destination in Latin America with extensive resort infrastructure. Race week activities centered at Blue Mall Punta Cana, which hosts the expo, pre-race group rides, start/finish line, and post-race activities.

history.origin_story: GFNY República Dominicana is part of the GFNY Global Endurance Sports Series, which operates 30+ cycling events worldwide across five continents. The event brings the 'Pro For A Day' concept to the Caribbean.
notable_moments: Part of GFNY's expansion to multiple continents with international peloton from nearly 100 nations
Established as a competitive and non-competitive racing option with separate course structures

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY Dominican Republic is a wind-defined race that rewards tactical patience and mental toughness. The outbound leg's false sense of ease—powered by favorable tailwind through beautiful rural terrain—sets up a humbling return journey. This is not a race to chase early moves; it's a race to conserve energy, read the wind, and unleash in the final 10km when others are suffering. The Caribbean setting elevates the experience beyond pure cycling metrics, and the professional race organization ensures riders feel like legitimate competitors. Best suited for cyclists who enjoy wind strategy and don't mind a challenging finish.

final_verdict.one_liner: A wind-dependent Caribbean challenge that tests tactical racing and mental resilience in a stunning tropical setting.
final_verdict.should_you_race: GFNY Dominican Republic delivers a unique racing experience where wind becomes the primary opponent rather than terrain. The 149.7km long course offers genuine competitive racing with a well-organized international field, while the 93.8km medium course provides an accessible alternative. The race's greatest strength is its strategic depth—the favorable outbound leg tempts aggressive racing, but the brutal headwind return punishes poor pacing decisions. Punta Cana's resort infrastructure, direct international flights, and beautiful Caribbean scenery make this an attractive destination race. However, the relatively modest elevation gain and lack of technical terrain limit its appeal for climbers or technical specialists. The race shines for cyclists seeking a professional racing experience in an exotic location, particularly those who thrive in wind-based tactical racing. Not ideal for first-time gran fondo participants or those seeking a pure climbing challenge, but excellent for experienced riders wanting to test their racing acumen in paradise.

videos.title: GFNY Republica Dominicana 2019
videos.description: The inaugural edition of GFNY Republica Dominicana will be held on April 7, 2019 in Punta Cana. Find out more at www.gfny.com
